mission.title: Install and Configure KubeRay
mission.description: KubeRay is an open-source Kubernetes operator designed to simplify the deployment and management of Ray applications on Kubernetes. It provides custom resource definitions for managing Ray clusters, jobs, and services, making it ideal for users looking to leverage Ray's capabilities in a Kubernetes environment. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts and potential complexity in managing resources.

steps.title: Add the KubeRay Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official KubeRay Helm repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeRay using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kuberay https://ray-project.github.io/kuberay

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the KubeRay Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeRay
steps.description:
This step installs KubeRay using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kuberay-system

helm install kuberay kuberay/kuberay --namespace kuberay-system --version 1.5.1

```

> Creates a namespace for KubeRay resources.
> Installs KubeRay in the specified namespace.

steps.title: Verify KubeRay installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the KubeRay components are running correctly in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay-system

kubectl get crd

```

> Lists all pods in the KubeRay namespace to verify they are running.
> Lists all custom resource definitions to ensure KubeRay CRDs are installed.

steps.title: Create a RayCluster
steps.description:
This step creates a RayCluster custom resource to deploy a Ray cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: ray.io/v1
kind: RayCluster
metadata:
  name: my-ray-cluster
  namespace: kuberay-system
spec:
  head:
    replicas: 1
  worker:
    replicas: 2
EOF

```

> Creates a RayCluster with 1 head and 2 worker nodes.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: ray.io/v1
kind: RayCluster
metadata:
  name: my-ray-cluster
  namespace: kuberay-system
spec:
  head:
    replicas: 1
  worker:
    replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify RayCluster status
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the created RayCluster to ensure it is running as expected.

```bash

kubectl get raycluster my-ray-cluster -n kuberay-system

```

> Displays the status of the RayCluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeRay
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeRay and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all RayClusters and their data.

```bash

helm uninstall kuberay --namespace kuberay-system

kubectl delete namespace kuberay-system

```

> Uninstalls KubeRay from the cluster.
> Deletes the KubeRay namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeRay
upgrade.description:
This step outlines the process to upgrade KubeRay to a newer version, including backup and verification.

```bash

kubectl get raycluster my-ray-cluster -n kuberay-system -o yaml > my-ray-cluster-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kuberay kuberay/kuberay --namespace kuberay-system --version 1.5.1

kubectl get pods -n kuberay-system

```

> Backs up the current RayCluster configuration.
> Upgrades KubeRay to the specified version.
> Verifies that all KubeRay pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get raycluster my-ray-cluster -n kuberay-system -o yaml > my-ray-cluster-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error creating RayCluster: Failed to create head pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if there are insufficient resources in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your cluster has enough resources (minimum 2 vCPU, 4GB RAM per node).

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'RayCluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the RayCluster pods are not in a running state.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kuberay-system' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Ray cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Ray head pod is not accessible.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure the head pod is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl apply failed: no matches for kind 'RayCluster' in version 'ray.io/v1'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the KubeRay CRDs are not installed.

**Fix:**
Reinstall KubeRay using the Helm install command to ensure CRDs are created.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Ray features and improved stability of RayCluster management.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No special migration steps required when upgrading from v1.5.0.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME              READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE' when running 'kubectl get pods -n kuberay-system'. All pods should be in the 'Running' state.

  worker:

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources and the necessary tools installed.
